meaning: adj.(尤指风、举止、仪表等)上等的,优等的,漂亮的
examples: The German star put in a classy performance.
She chose to be backed by a classy trio of acoustic guitar, bass and congas.
The classy piano bar next to Maddalena's really sells 11 dishes for the advertised price at lunch. There's no bait and switch here.
" The one he wore on Wednesday was "classy, " he explained, and "very rare.
The new owner could wind up owning a classy yacht for 60 cents on the dollar.
